Query: TPS device is not booting up and it booted from compact flash after power cycle. How to address this?
Context: The TPS device booting from Compact Flash indicates a problem with the hard drive. You can try to fix the boot region of the hard drive by selecting option 9 in the Restore menu. If this doesn't work, you may need to reformat and restore the hard drive by selecting option 8 in the same menu. Please ensure to backup any important data before proceeding as this will erase all data on the hard drive.
Answer: The TPS device booting from Compact Flash indicates a problem with the hard drive. You can try to fix the boot region of the hard drive by selecting option 9 in the Restore menu. If this doesn't work, you may need to reformat and restore the hard drive by selecting option 8 in the same menu. Please ensure to backup any important data before proceeding as this will erase all data on the hard drive.

Query: We saw a trunk flap in TPS device. What can the root cause of this flap?
Context: The root cause of the trunk flap in the TPS device could be due to the reduction in buffers after upgrading the TH1040-bypass from 3.2.5-P5 to 5.0.3-TPS. This was indicated by the buffer drop logs and the analysis which showed a decrease in buffers from 1.6M to 700K in the upgraded version.
Answer: The root cause of the trunk flap in the TPS device could be due to the reduction in buffers after upgrading the TH1040-bypass from 3.2.5-P5 to 5.0.3-TPS. This was indicated by the buffer drop logs and the analysis which showed a decrease in buffers from 1.6M to 700K in the upgraded version.

Query: TPS blacklist a DNS query with a UDP filter and CEF logs but will not create logs.
Context: The issue of TPS not creating a log when blacklisting a DNS query with a UDP filter and CEF logs has been identified as a bug with the ID ACOS-43146. The resolution for this bug will be included in the upcoming version 5.0.4.

Query: Why does vThunder gets hung when performing a snapshot on ESXi?
Context: The issue of vThunder getting hung when performing a snapshot on ESXi is a known limitation. The VM may become unresponsive when taking a snapshot. This is likely due to the quiesced snapshot causing the VM to be unresponsive. A workaround for this can be to go for a generic VM memory snapshot instead of a quiesced snapshot.
Answer: The issue of vThunder getting hung when performing a snapshot on ESXi is a known limitation. The VM may become unresponsive when taking a snapshot. This is likely due to the quiesced snapshot causing the VM to be unresponsive. A workaround for this can be to go for a generic VM memory snapshot instead of a quiesced snapshot.
